philosophy:

Continental versus Analytic?

Atheism (No belief in God(-s))

Theism (Belief in God(-s))

Meta-ethics
Aim of philosophy (which is most important?): wisdom, understanding, truth/knowledge, happiness, or goodness/justice?

Is the mind:
Non-physical (like a soul, which is
separate from the body, and can survive the body's death is) 

or

Physical (the mind is the brain or the mind is a physical thing that fits the right criteria such as the right functional organisation)

Realism (There is an objective world, and the goal of science is to provide a true description of reality.)

or 

Anti-Realism (Science does not necessarily have to provide a true description of the objective world.)

What is your preferred interpretation of probability?

Bayesian (The probability of an event is interpreted as reasonable expectation and updated as information updates)

or

Frequentist (The probability of an event is its relative frequency over time)

libertarianism, no free will, or compatibilism?

Veganism (no and no), omnivorism (yes and yes), or vegetarianism (no and yes)

Empiricism (True knowledge comes from sensory experience and empirical evidence)

or

Rationalism (Knowledge can be derived from reason; Reason is the chef source of knowledge)

Capitalism or Socialism?

or

unreal, social, psychological, or biological?

Immortality: Would you choose it?
Is knowledge Justified True Belief (JTB)?
Aesthetic experience: Is art a kind of sui generis (unique experience), perception, or pleasure?
The Trolley Problem

The Ship of Theseus:  Identity Over Time (Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy)
"If an object really changes, then there can’t literally be one and the same thing before and after the change.
If there isn’t literally one and the same object before and after a change, then no one object has really undergone any change."

The Ship of Theseus over the course of a hundred years undergoes repairs and renovation. At the end of the hundred years every component on the ship is distinct from the original creation. At the end of the hundred years, is it still The Ship of Theseus?

Philosophical Zombies (P-Zombies): 
"The philosophical notion of a “zombie” basically refers to conceivable creatures which are physically indistinguishable from us but lack consciousness entirely." 
Consciousness (Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy)

What is your opinion on P-zombies?

The Experience Machine: The Experience Machine (Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy)

Imagine a machine that could give you any experience (or sequence of experiences) you might desire. 

You can program your experiences for tomorrow, or this week, or this year, or even for the rest of your life.  You can live your fondest dreams “from the inside.” 

Once you enter, you will not remember choosing to do so. Will you choose to enter the machine for the rest of your life?
Wake up, you're in a simulation!
If you can go back in time, would you kill baby Hitler?
Do you really subscribe to the principle of charity?
Is Alt a dictator
